name: notebooklm-slides
description:
Generate pedagogically-aligned slide decks from educational content using NotebookLM.
Use when creating chapter slide presentations with proficiency-calibrated prompts.
NOT for static slides or non-educational presentations.
capabilities: Generate slide decks calibrated to CEFR levels (A2 beginner to C1 advanced)
Create pedagogically-aligned presentations with 3-7 bullet points per slide
Structure narratives following problem → transformation → opportunity → action arc
Provide ready-to-use prompts for NotebookLM browser automation
Validate slide output against 7 success criteria gates
author: 92Bilal26
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation and structured prompts. No executable code exists in this skill. The static scanner incorrectly flagged content hash strings as cryptographic code and bash documentation examples as shell execution commands. The skill only provides educational slide generation workflows through browser automation prompts.
valueStatement: Creating chapter slides is time-consuming and often produces generic, text-heavy presentations. This skill provides proficiency-calibrated prompts for NotebookLM to generate pedagogically-aligned slide decks with clear narrative arcs and actionable endings.
limitations: Requires NotebookLM account and daily generation limits apply (3-5 chapters per day)
Requires browser-use skill for automated slide generation workflow
Does not generate slides directly; provides prompts that users submit to NotebookLM

useCases.title: Create Chapter Presentations
useCases.description: Generate slide decks for each chapter with content matched to student proficiency levels

useCases.title: Build Training Materials
useCases.description: Create detailed C1-level slides with architecture diagrams and decision matrices

useCases.title: Develop Beginner Courses
useCases.description: Produce encouraging A2-level presentations with simple language and actionable endings

promptTemplates.title: A2 Beginner Slides
promptTemplates.prompt: Create inspiring slide deck for absolute beginners (A2 proficiency). AUDIENCE: Complete beginners with no programming experience. Include 3-5 principles: simple explanation, accessible mental model, encouraging tone. 12-15 slides with 3-5 bullet points each. END WITH: Specific next steps.

promptTemplates.title: B1 Intermediate Slides
promptTemplates.prompt: Create comprehensive slide deck for intermediate learners (B1 proficiency). Include problem-solving approaches and real-world applications. 15-20 slides with practical examples. END WITH: Implementation strategies.

promptTemplates.title: C1 Advanced Slides
promptTemplates.prompt: Create detailed slide deck for advanced practitioners (C1 proficiency). Include theoretical frameworks, industry patterns, and critical analysis. 20-25 slides with architecture diagrams. END WITH: Production deployment strategies.

promptTemplates.title: Batch Chapter Processing
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate slides for multiple chapters efficiently
promptTemplates.prompt: Process chapters 1-5 using batch workflow. Create notebooks first, upload all sources, then generate sequentially. Track daily limits. Download and rename each slide deck as chapter-NN-slides.pdf. Validate each against 7 success criteria.

bestPractices: Always specify proficiency level (A2, B1, C1) and provide concrete audience description
Include explicit slide format requirements (bullet count, slide range, NOT paragraphs)
Validate generated slides against all 7 success criteria before deployment
antiPatterns: Using vague audience descriptions like beginners instead of specific levels
Skipping framework principles results in generic, non-pedagogical output
Leaving slide format as default produces text-heavy, ineffective presentations

faq.question: What proficiency levels are supported?
faq.answer: A2 (beginners), B1 (intermediate), and C1 (advanced) with calibrated prompts for each level.

faq.question: How many slides does each level generate?
faq.answer: A2: 12-15 slides, B1: 15-20 slides, C1: 20-25 slides with varying bullet depths.

faq.question: Does this skill integrate with other tools?
faq.answer: Yes, it requires the browser-use skill for browser automation and NotebookLM web interface.

faq.question: Is my educational content safe?
faq.answer: Content is only uploaded to NotebookLM via browser. No data is stored or accessed by this skill.

faq.question: Why did my generation get stuck?
faq.answer: Check NotebookLM daily limits (3-5 chapters per day) or browser console for error messages.

faq.question: How is this different from other slide tools?
faq.answer: NotebookLM generates pedagogically-aligned content with proficiency-calibrated language and narrative structure.
